texto: The greatest lesson for democracies to learn is for the majority to give to the minority a full, free opportunity to present their side of the case, and then for the minority, having failed to win a majority to their views, gracefully to submit and to recognize the action as that of the entire organization, and cheerfully to assist in carrying it out until they can secure its repeal.
explicacao: Citação de Henry Martyn Robert sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “The greatest lesson for democracies to learn is for the majority to give to the minority a full, …” articula sabedoria, democracy, parliamentarism em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como parliamentarism e political-philosophy.

frase_original: The greatest lesson for democracies to learn is for the majority to give to the minority a full, free opportunity to present their side of the case, and then for the minority, having failed to win a majority to their views, gracefully to submit and to recognize the action as that of the entire organization, and cheerfully to assist in carrying it out until they can secure its repeal.
